calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2026 Continued To Sep-02-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-23-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT DAVID STEPHENSON, STEPHENSON NETWORKS, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings

Defendant David Stephenson and Stephenson Networks, Inc.'s Motion To Stay Proceedings is GRANTED.

The instant action is a coverage action for declaratory relief regarding whether the policy between Plaintiff and Defendants covers particular claims. The particular claims are the subject of andinextricably intertwined with the facts and issues in play in 1100 Sacramento Street Homeowners Association v. Park Lane Associates, L.P, Case No. CGC-22-600509 ("Underlying Action"). The Underlying Action is ongoing.

If there are overlapping factual issues to be resolved in the coverage action and in the underlying action, "then a stay is mandatory." (See Riddell, Inc. v. Superior Court (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 755, 771 (emphasis added) (citing Great American Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 221, 235); see also United Enterprises, Inc. v. Superior Court (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1004,1010 ("because factual issues to be resolved in the declaratory relief action overlap factual issues to be resolved in the underlying actions, the court was required to issue the stay.") The court finds there are overlapping factual issues in this case and the Underlying Action. Thus, the court stays this action.

The court also exercises its discretion in favor of a stay. In the absence of factual overlap between the declaratory relief action and the underlying action, "the question whether to grant a stay or fashion some other remedy is left to the discretion of the trial court." (United Enterprises, supra, 183 Cal.App.4th at p. 1012.) A discretionary stay may be appropriate if otherwise the insured would have to fight "a two-front war, doing battle with the plaintiffs in the [underlying action] while at the same time devoting its money and its human resources to litigating coverage issues with its carriers." (Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 902, 910-911; United Enterprises, supra, 183 Cal.App.4th at p. 1012.) Prejudice is a significant consideration as well. (Great American Insurance, supra, 178 Cal.App.4th 236-237.) Here, absent a stay, Defendants would, in fact, have to fight a two-front war and battle victories in one could substantially contribute (if not inextricably lead) to losses on the other. Further, on balance, Defendants are likely to suffer greater prejudice absent a stay than Plaintiff is likely to suffer in the event of a stay. Plaintiff's arguments to the contrary are unavailing.

The court orders this action stayed pending resolution of the Underlying Action or further order of this court.

order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn: Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
